<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Imi-chet-Sopdet]]
{{Infobox hieroglyphs
|NAME = <hiero>G17-M3:Aa1*X1-S29-M44-X1 N14-N33</hiero> 
|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = 
|NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Imi-chet-Sopdet<br/>''Jmj-ḫt-Spd.t''<br/>''Follower of {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}}''
|BILD1 = Position_Alpha_Cmi.png 
|BILD1-BREITE = 250px
|BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation {{ill|Canis Minor|en|Canis Minor|qid=Q9305|lt=Canis Minor|1=Canis Minor}}
}}

'''Imi-chet-Sopdet''', an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} [[Baktiu|decan star]] designated as ''G'', corresponds to the star {{ill|Procyon|en|Procyon|qid=Q13034|lt=Procyon|1=Procyon}} (α Canis Minor) in the modern constellation {{ill|Canis Minor|en|Canis Minor|qid=Q9305|lt=Canis Minor|1=Canis Minor}}. The name ''Follower of Sopdet'' was given because Imi-chet-Sopdet [[Culmination (astronomy)|culminates]] 10 days after [[Sopdet (constellation)|Sopdet]] and 20 days after [[Tepi-a-Sopdet]], despite the fact that Procyon rises before {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}} in the sky. In antiquity, Procyon was often regarded, even outside Egypt, as the "companion arrow star of Sirius."

== Rektaszension ==
* '''2101 BCE''': 60°.70 

== Literature ==
* {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 102.  
* [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5
{{translated page|de|Imi-chet-Sopdet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}



[[de:Imi-chet-Sopdet]]

<!--Imi-chet-Sopdet--><!--Imi-chet-Sopdet--><!--Imi-chet-Sopdet-->

<!--dewiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q1272698}}
[[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
